title: 10 best herbal capsules for chronic hepatitis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for chronic hepatitis are dietary supplements that contain a combination of herbs and other natural ingredients, designed to support liver health and function. These capsules can help reduce inflammation, improve liver enzyme levels, and enhance overall well-being in individuals with chronic hepatitis. Some examples of herbal ingredients commonly found in these capsules include milk thistle, turmeric, schisandra, and dandelion root. These herbs have been shown to provide antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis and potentially slow down the progression of the disease. By incorporating herbal capsules into their treatment plan, many individuals with chronic hepatitis have reported improved energy levels, reduced fatigue, and a better quality of life. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These compounds, such as glycyrrhizin, can help reduce liver inflammation, protect liver cells from damage, and inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. Additionally, licorice has been shown to improve liver function tests and reduce symptoms such as fatigue and nausea in individuals with chronic hepatitis. However, it is important to note that licorice should only be taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as high doses or long-term use can have side effects.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice helps reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and potentially slow the progression of the disease. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Hepatoprotective: Licorice has a protective effect on liver cells, helping to prevent further damage and promote healing. This is crucial for chronic hepatitis as it can help improve liver function and reduce the risk of complications. The key active constituents include glycyrrhizin, liquiritin, and isoliquiritigenin.

Antiviral: Licorice has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. This is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help reduce the viral load and potentially slow the progression of the disease. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Immunomodulatory: Licorice can help regulate the immune response, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help reduce inflammation and prevent further liver damage. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Licorice has antioxidant properties, which can help protect liver cells from oxidative stress and damage. This is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and chalcones.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2000mg of dried licorice root and grind it into a fine powder, typically taking 15-20 minutes.
Measure 500mg of the licorice root powder and mix it with an excipient, such as microcrystalline cellulose, to improve capsule consistency and fill 00-sized empty gelatin capsules.
Fill the gelatin capsules with the licorice root powder and excipient mixture, usually taking 10-15 minutes per 100 capsules.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place to ensure proper sealing and stability.
Store the dried, filled licorice root capsules in an airtight container, preferably in a cool, dark location, for up to six months.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-viral properties, making it useful for treating chronic hepatitis.

Seeds: Licorice seeds are rich in flavonoids and coumarins, which have been shown to protect the liver and support liver function, making them a good choice for medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant contain various compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects and may help to reduce liver inflammation.

Flowers: Licorice flowers are rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect liver cells from damage and support liver function, making them a valuable addition to medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsule helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that supports liver health. This compound helps to protect the liver from damage and promotes the regeneration of liver cells. Milk thistle has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve liver function in people with chronic hepatitis, making it a valuable supplement for those looking to manage this condition. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also help to reduce oxidative stress and protect the liver from further damage. Additionally, milk thistle has been shown to help reduce liver enzyme levels, a key indicator of liver health, in individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and slow down the progression of the disease. This property is due to the active constituent, silymarin.

Antioxidant: Milk thistle has antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver damage. The active constituent responsible for this property is also silymarin.

Hepatoprotective: Milk thistle has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning it can help protect the liver from toxins and damage. This is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help support liver function and prevent further liver damage. The active constituent responsible for this property is silymarin.

Immune-modulating: Milk thistle can help regulate the immune system, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help reduce inflammation and prevent further liver damage. The active constituents responsible for this property are silymarin and silybin.

Anti-fibrotic: Milk thistle has been shown to have anti-fibrotic effects, meaning it can help prevent the formation of scar tissue in the liver. This is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help prevent the progression of the disease to cirrhosis. The active constituent responsible for this property is silymarin.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried milk thistle seeds and a gelatin capsule maker, ensuring the seeds are organic and the capsules are suitable for consumption.
Grind 200 grams of milk thistle seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill the gelatin capsules with the milk thistle seed powder using the capsule maker, creating 100 capsules with 200mg of powder per capsule.
Store the completed milk thistle capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place to maintain potency.

Consult a healthcare professional for dosage recommendations and to ensure there are no potential interactions with existing medications or health conditions. Preparation time will depend on the time required to source the ingredients and the time needed to grind and encapsulate the seeds, typically taking 1-2 hours.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant contain silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have liver-protecting properties, making them useful for managing chronic hepatitis.

Leaves: Milk thistle leaves are a source of antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and support liver function, making them a potential component in medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis.

Flowers: The flowers of the milk thistle plant contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may be beneficial in managing chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. Curcumin helps reduce inflammation in the liver, a key factor in hepatitis. It also helps protect liver cells from damage and promotes the regeneration of healthy liver tissue. Moreover, curcumin has been shown to inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses, further supporting its use in managing chronic hepatitis. By addressing multiple aspects of hepatitis, turmeric capsules offer a holistic approach to managing this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Curcumin, the main active ingredient in turmeric, can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and slow down the progression of the disease.

Antioxidant: Turmeric has strong antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help reduce oxidative stress and support liver function.

Hepatoprotective: Turmeric has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, which means it can help protect the liver from damage and promote its regeneration, making it important for chronic hepatitis as it can help improve liver health and function.

Antiviral: Turmeric has antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, making it important for chronic hepatitis as it can help reduce the viral load and slow down the progression of the disease.

Immune-modulatory: Turmeric has immune-modulatory effects, which means it can help regulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and damage to liver cells. This is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and support liver function.
remedy_recipe: Purchase or grow organic turmeric root, organic black pepper, and a vegetarian capsule-making kit, ensuring the turmeric powder contains at least 95% curcumin.
Clean and dry the turmeric root, then grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or blender, and mix in a pinch of black pepper powder to enhance curcumin absorption.
Measure out 500mg of the turmeric-black pepper mixture for each capsule, filling the capsule halves according to the kit's instructions, and store the completed capsules in an airtight container.
Consult a healthcare professional to determine the recommended daily dosage, typically starting at 500mg up to three times daily, and adjust as necessary based on individual response.
Store the container in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, and use within three months for optimal potency.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis due to their high curcumin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the liver.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain curcuminoids, which have been shown to have potential therapeutic effects in liver diseases, including chronic hepatitis.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their hepatoprotective properties, which may help prevent or treat liver damage caused by chronic hepatitis.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers contain curcuminoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful for making medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis.

Seeds: Turmeric seeds, also known as turmeric bulbs, have been used in traditional medicine for their liver-protecting properties, making them a potential ingredient for medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Baical skullcap capsule helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can potentially improve liver function. The herb's active compounds, such as baicalin and wogonin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses and reduce liver inflammation. Additionally, baical skullcap may help protect liver cells from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to the progression of chronic hepatitis. However, it is important to note that baical skullcap should be used as a complementary therapy and should not replace any prescribed medication or medical treatment for chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Baical skullcap capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and slow down the progression of the disease. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids such as baicalein and wogonin.

Antiviral: Baical skullcap has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat the hepatitis virus, a key factor in chronic hepatitis. The antiviral effects are attributed to the flavonoids and other active constituents like baicalin.

Hepatoprotective: Baical skullcap can protect liver cells from damage, which is crucial in managing chronic hepatitis. This property is attributed to the flavonoids, particularly baicalin and wogonin, which help maintain the structural integrity of liver cells.

Antioxidant: Baical skullcap has strong antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress in the liver, a common issue in chronic hepatitis. The antioxidant effects are due to the presence of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds.

Immunomodulatory: Baical skullcap can help regulate the immune system, which is important for chronic hepatitis as an overactive immune response can contribute to liver damage. This property is attributed to the flavonoids and other active constituents, which can help balance the immune system and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried baical skullcap herb and 1 teaspoon of crushed granulated microcrystalline cellulose as a binding agent.
Mix the herb and cellulose thoroughly in a small bowl.
Add a few drops of distilled water to the mixture, just enough to make it stick together, and blend well to form a uniform paste.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the paste using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 24 hours before storing in an airtight container.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Baical skullcap plant contain high levels of flavonoids, which have been shown to have liver-protecting properties, making them useful for treating chronic hepatitis.
Flowers: The flowers of the Baical skullcap plant contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and protect liver cells from damage, making them beneficial for managing chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the Baical skullcap plant are rich in essential oils and fatty acids that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, making them useful for treating chronic hepatitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the Baical skullcap plant contain high levels of flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making them useful for managing chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsule helps with chronic hepatitis because it promotes liver health. The plant contains bioactive compounds, such as taraxacin, taraxacerin, and inulin, which have been shown to support liver function and protect against damage. Dandelion is also a rich source of antioxidants, including vitamin C and polyphenols, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver. Furthermore, dandelion has been shown to increase bile production and flow, which aids in the digestion of fats and the elimination of toxins from the body. These properties make dandelion capsules a valuable supplement for individuals with chronic hepatitis, as they can help to alleviate symptoms and promote overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and potentially slow the progression of the disease. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids such as luteolin and taraxasterol.

Hepatoprotective: Dandelion capsules can protect liver cells from damage, which is crucial for chronic hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver dysfunction. The active constituents responsible for this property include taraxacin, taraxacoside, and caffeic acid.

Antioxidant: Dandelion capsules have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress in the liver, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help protect liver cells from damage. The antioxidants in dandelion include vitamin C, vitamin E, and various flavonoids.

Diuretic: Dandelion capsules can help increase urine production, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help eliminate toxins from the body and reduce the workload on the liver. The active constituents responsible for this property include taraxacin, taraxacoside, and potassium.

Immune-boosting: Dandelion capsules can help boost the immune system, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help the body fight off infections and promote healing. The immune-boosting properties of dandelion are due to the presence of various nutrients, including vitamins A, C, and B complex, as well as minerals such as iron, calcium, and magnesium.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dandelion plants from an organic, pesticide-free source, ensuring the flowers, leaves, and roots are intact; cleanly rinse and dry the herbs, then finely chop and combine equal parts of each component.

Set the oven to a low temperature of 200°F (93°C) and evenly distribute the chopped dandelion mixture on a baking sheet, allowing it to dry for approximately 2 hours or until the herbs become crisp and crumble easily.
Grind the dried dandelion mixture into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or blender, passing it through a sieve to ensure a consistent texture and removing any larger particles.

Measure out 500 milligrams of the ground dandelion powder for each capsule, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand to fill size "00" vegetarian capsules, tamping down the powder to remove any air pockets.
Seal the filled capsules and store in an airtight container in a cool, dark place; take 2-3 capsules daily or as directed by a healthcare professional for managing chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are commonly used in medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the liver.
Leaves: The leaves of dandelions are rich in vitamins and minerals, and have been shown to improve liver function, making them a valuable addition to medicinal capsules.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making them a popular choice for medicinal capsules.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds are a rich source of essential fatty acids and have been shown to have a positive impact on liver health, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry capsule helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have potential therapeutic effects on liver diseases. Berberine has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation, protect liver cells from damage, and inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses. Additionally, barberry has been used traditionally to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, further supporting its potential benefits for individuals with chronic hepatitis. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Barberry contains berberine, which helps reduce inflammation in the liver, alleviating chronic hepatitis symptoms.
Hepatoprotective: The antioxidant properties of barberry help protect liver cells from damage, promoting recovery in chronic hepatitis.
Antiviral: Barberry's berberine and alkaloids exhibit antiviral effects, inhibiting hepatitis virus replication.
Immune-boosting: Barberry stimulates the immune system, enhancing the body's ability to fight off the hepatitis virus.
Cholagogue: Barberry promotes bile secretion, aiding in the removal of toxins from the liver and supporting overall liver function.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried common barberry bark and steep in 8 ounces of boiling water for 15-20 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the liquid, pressing the barberry bark to extract all herbal components, and let it cool down.
Mix 1 gram of the cooled decoction with an appropriate binder and excipient to form a slurry, following pharmaceutical guidelines for capsule production.
Fill size "00" gelatin capsules with the slurry using a capsule filling machine or manual method, ensuring consistent weight and size.
Dry the filled capsules in a controlled environment with adequate ventilation for 24-48 hours before storing in airtight containers away from light and heat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the common barberry plant contain berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating chronic hepatitis.

Bark: The bark of the barberry plant is rich in berberine and other compounds that have been shown to have a protective effect on the liver, making it a valuable component in medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis.
Berries: The berries of the barberry plant contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help to protect liver cells from damage and support liver function in individuals with chronic hepatitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the barberry plant contain berberine and other compounds that have been shown to have a positive effect on liver enzyme levels, making them a useful addition to medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Katuki capsule helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains a potent herbal extract derived from the Picrorhiza kurroa plant. This extract has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to treat liver ailments. The main active components in Katuki, such as kutkin and picroside, have been shown to possess potent hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce liver inflammation, protect liver cells from damage, and improve overall liver function. Furthermore, Katuki has been found to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in digestion and detoxification processes. These mechanisms of action make Katuki an effective adjunct therapy for managing chronic hepatitis, providing support to the liver and promoting its recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Katuki capsules have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation, a key factor in chronic hepatitis, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides like swertiamarin and sweroside.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Katuki capsules, attributed to its phenolic constituents, help protect liver cells from oxidative damage, which is prevalent in chronic hepatitis.

Hepatoprotective: Katuki capsules exhibit hepatoprotective effects, which means they can safeguard the liver from toxins, thus aiding in the treatment of chronic hepatitis, primarily because of the presence of gentianine and secoiridoids.
Antiviral: Katuki capsules possess antiviral properties that can help combat the viral infections causing chronic hepatitis, thanks to the presence of xanthones like mangiferin.
Immunomodulatory: Katuki capsules can modulate the immune system, helping to control the overactive immune response in chronic hepatitis, due to the presence of alkaloids like gentianine.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of Katuki root to a fine powder, which may take up to 30 minutes.
Mix the Katuki powder with 150 grams of excipient, such as microcrystalline cellulose, to improve flow properties and capsule filling.
Fill size "00" hypromellose capsules with the Katuki-excipient mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand, to produce approximately 250 capsules.
Store the Katuki capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, to preserve their potency and quality.
Recommend patients to take 2-3 Katuki capsules, two times a day, 30 minutes before meals, for at least three months for chronic hepatitis treatment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain a high concentration of active compounds, making them effective in treating liver diseases.
Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants, they help reduce inflammation and protect the liver from damage.
Leaves: Packed with essential oils and nutrients, they support liver function and promote healing.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that have hepatoprotective properties, aiding in the treatment of chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, they help improve liver health and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Andrographis capsule helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains Andrographis paniculata, a herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common in chronic hepatitis. Studies have shown that Andrographis extract can help improve liver enzyme levels and reduce symptoms of hepatitis, such as fatigue and liver tenderness. Additionally, Andrographis has been found to have antiviral properties, which may help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. Overall, Andrographis capsules may provide a natural and effective complementary treatment option for individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Andrographis capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation, a key factor in chronic hepatitis, due to the presence of diterpene lactones like andrographolide.

Antiviral: Andrographis capsules exhibit antiviral properties that can help inhibit hepatitis virus replication, slowing down the progression of chronic hepatitis, attributed to the active constituent andrographolide.

Hepatoprotective: Andrographis capsules protect the liver from damage caused by chronic hepatitis through antioxidant effects, preserving liver function and promoting recovery, due to the presence of flavonoids and other polyphenols.

Immune-modulating: Andrographis capsules can help regulate the immune system, reducing excessive immune responses that contribute to liver damage in chronic hepatitis, owing to the active constituents like andrographolide and diterpene glycosides.

Antifibrotic: Andrographis capsules possess antifibrotic properties that can help prevent or reduce liver fibrosis, a common complication of chronic hepatitis, by inhibiting the production of extracellular matrix components, due to the active constituents like andrographolide and deoxyandrographolide.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried Andrographis paniculata herb and vegetarian capsules with a size 00 size.
Grind 2 grams of dried Andrographis paniculata herb into a fine powder, which should take around 5 minutes.
Measure 500mg of the Andrographis paniculata powder and fill each capsule, which should take around 10 minutes.
Repeat step 3 until you have filled all the capsules, ensuring they are properly sealed.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the andrographis plant are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Leaves: The leaves of the andrographis plant are used in medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis because they contain high levels of andrographolides, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects.

Flowers: The flowers of the andrographis plant are used in medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.

Seeds: The seeds of the andrographis plant are used in medicinal capsules for chronic hepatitis because they contain high levels of andrographolides, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects.

This herbal remedy contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation, protect liver cells from damage, and potentially inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Stonebreaker capsules contain antioxidants that help protect liver cells from oxidative stress, which is important in managing chronic hepatitis as it can reduce inflammation and damage to liver tissues. The active constituents responsible for these antioxidant properties include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Hepatoprotective properties: Stonebreaker capsules have been shown to protect the liver from damage, which is crucial in managing chronic hepatitis as it can help prevent further progression of the disease. remedy_recipe: Purchase dried stonebreaker herb (Phyllanthus niruri) and measure out 2 teaspoons (4 grams) for each capsule serving.
Grind the dried stonebreaker herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Load the ground herb into size "00" empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times a day, 30 minutes before meals, for a total of 3-6 capsules per day. Consult with a healthcare provider for appropriate dosage and duration.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried greater celandine herb, ensuring it is properly identified and sourced from a reputable supplier.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chronic hepatitis with herbal capsules is by using a combination of milk thistle, turmeric, and artichoke extract. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that protect the liver from further damage and promote healing. Milk thistle, in particular, is known for its ability to regenerate liver cells. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chronic hepatitis that are treated with herbal capsules include liver cirrhosis and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. Milk thistle is a commonly used herb that has been shown to improve liver function and protect against further damage. Other herbs that may be used in herbal capsules for liver conditions include dandelion root, turmeric, and artichoke extract. These herbs are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, as herbal supplements can interact with other medications and may not be appropriate for everyone.
